title: Fail closed authorization
description: Authorization decisions should deny when identity, ownership, tenant, scope, or policy evidence is missing.
body: Use when changing route guards, object access, feature entitlements, admin checks, service-to-service permissions, or multi-tenant data reads. Do not use to invent a business permission model; defer to the project policy when explicit rules exist. Actions: make the allow path explicit, keep missing context as deny, and add tests for unauthenticated, wrong-role, wrong-owner, and wrong-tenant cases. Validation signals include negative security tests, rejected requests with stable error shape, and no sensitive object in the response or log. Risk boundary: any fallback from deny to allow, break-glass bypass, or policy exception needs human approval and an audit trail.
